description: Comprehensive product buildout: security hardening, image editor (inspired by Canva clone patterns using Fabric.js), content planner/calendar, blog/newsletter publishing adapters (Substack, Beehiiv, Medium, Ghost), and platform polish. Tiered model assignment: haiku for boilerplate, sonnet for moderate features, opus for complex architecture.

userStories.title: Add account ownership check to publish API
userStories.description: In apps/content-os/src/app/api/distribution/publish/route.ts, after loading accounts by accountIds, verify each account's userId matches the requesting user. Return 403 for unauthorized accounts. This prevents users from posting to accounts they don't own.
acceptanceCriteria: Publish API validates account ownership before creating distribution jobs
Returns 403 with clear error message for unauthorized accounts
Legitimate publish requests still succeed
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Replace window.prompt with modal in accounts page
userStories.description: In apps/content-os/src/app/accounts/page.tsx, replace window.prompt() calls for credential input with a proper React modal form. Use a controlled form with input validation. Never expose credentials in browser prompt dialogs.
acceptanceCriteria: No window.prompt() calls remain in accounts page
Modal form captures credentials with proper input types
Form validates required fields before submission
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Validate scheduledAt rejects past dates in publish route
userStories.description: In the publish API route, validate that scheduledAt is in the future. Return 400 if the date is in the past. Allow null scheduledAt for immediate publishing.
acceptanceCriteria: Past dates rejected with 400 error and descriptive message
Null scheduledAt still works for immediate publishing
Timezone-aware comparison using UTC
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Fix window.location.href to router.push in content list
userStories.description: In apps/content-os/src/app/content/page.tsx, replace window.location.href navigation with Next.js router.push() to preserve client-side routing and avoid full page reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: No window.location.href calls in content page
Navigation uses Next.js router.push()
Back button behavior preserved
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Fix timer memory leak in content/new page
userStories.description: In apps/content-os/src/app/content/new/page.tsx, the setInterval for auto-save is not cleaned up on component unmount. Add a useEffect cleanup function that clears the interval.
acceptanceCriteria: setInterval cleaned up in useEffect return function
No memory leak on page navigation
Auto-save still works while on page
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add queue creation UI with schedule builder
userStories.description: Create a modal or dedicated page for creating publishing queues. Include: distribution account selector (multi-select from connected accounts), schedule builder (pick days of week + time slots), timezone picker, queue name input. Submit to POST /api/queue. The 'Create your first queue' button currently has no handler.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue creation form accessible from queue page
Account selector shows only user's connected accounts
Schedule builder allows selecting days and times
Timezone picker included with user's default pre-selected
Successful creation navigates to new queue view
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add analytics error isolation per metric
userStories.description: In apps/content-os/src/app/analytics/page.tsx, wrap each metric fetch in independent try/catch so one failing metric doesn't break the entire dashboard. Add per-metric loading and error states.
acceptanceCriteria: Each metric section has independent loading state
One failing metric shows error without breaking others
Retry button per failed metric section
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement Substack publishing adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/substack.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter. Substack uses email-based publishing via their API. createPost: POST to Substack API with title, body (HTML), subtitle. Support draft vs published state. getMetrics: GET post stats (views, opens, clicks). validateCredentials: verify API token validity. Register in platforms/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: SubstackAdapter implements PlatformAdapter with platform='substack'
createPost publishes or drafts a newsletter post via Substack API
getMetrics returns post stats (views, opens, clicks)
validateCredentials verifies API token
Registered in platforms/index.ts
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement Beehiiv publishing adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/beehiiv.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter. Beehiiv has a REST API (api.beehiiv.com/v2). createPost: POST /publications/{pubId}/posts with title, content (HTML), status (draft/confirmed/archived). getMetrics: GET /publications/{pubId}/posts/{id} for stats. validateCredentials: GET /publications to verify API key. Register in platforms/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: BeehiivAdapter implements PlatformAdapter with platform='beehiiv'
createPost creates a newsletter post via Beehiiv REST API
getMetrics returns post engagement stats
validateCredentials verifies API key by listing publications
Registered in platforms/index.ts
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement Medium publishing adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/medium.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter. Medium API uses OAuth2 + REST. getAuthUrl: build Medium OAuth URL. exchangeCode: POST to medium.com/v1/tokens. createPost: POST /v1/users/{userId}/posts with title, content (HTML/markdown), contentFormat, publishStatus, tags. getMetrics: Medium API doesn't expose metrics — return zeroed metrics with note. deletePost: not supported by API — throw descriptive error. Register in platforms/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: MediumAdapter implements PlatformAdapter with platform='medium'
OAuth flow generates correct Medium authorization URL
createPost publishes article with title, content, tags
getMetrics returns zeroed metrics with explanation (API limitation)
Registered in platforms/index.ts
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Implement Ghost publishing adapter
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/infrastructure/distribution/platforms/ghost.adapter.ts implementing PlatformAdapter. Ghost Admin API uses JWT auth (Admin API key → JWT token). createPost: POST /ghost/api/admin/posts with title, html, status (draft/published), tags. deletePost: DELETE /ghost/api/admin/posts/{id}. getMetrics: not available via Admin API — return zeroed metrics. validateCredentials: GET /ghost/api/admin/site to verify. Support self-hosted Ghost URLs via credentials.metadata.apiUrl. Register in platforms/index.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: GhostAdapter implements PlatformAdapter with platform='ghost'
JWT auth generated from Admin API key
createPost publishes/drafts to Ghost instance
Supports self-hosted Ghost via configurable API URL
validateCredentials verifies against /site endpoint
Registered in platforms/index.ts
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add Fabric.js canvas editor foundation
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/app/editor/page.tsx with a Fabric.js-powered canvas editor. Implement the 3-pane layout inspired by Canva clone: left sidebar (tool panels), center canvas (Fabric.js), right panel (layer properties). Include: canvas initialization with configurable dimensions, zoom/pan controls, undo/redo via Fabric.js state snapshots, JSON serialization for save/load. This is the foundation — individual tools added in subsequent stories.
acceptanceCriteria: Editor page renders 3-pane layout at /editor route
Fabric.js canvas initializes with configurable dimensions
Zoom and pan controls work
Undo/redo stores canvas state snapshots
Canvas state serializable to/from JSON
Sidebar has tabbed panel structure ready for tools
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add text tool to image editor
userStories.description: Add text tool to the Fabric.js editor. Implement: text layer creation with configurable font family, size, color, weight, style, alignment. Text editing via double-click on canvas. Font selector with Google Fonts integration (load via API). Text presets (heading, subheading, body) in sidebar panel. Config-driven approach: text presets defined in a config file, not hardcoded in components.
acceptanceCriteria: Text tool adds editable text objects to canvas
Double-click enables inline text editing
Property panel shows font controls when text selected
Font family selector loads Google Fonts
Text presets (heading/subheading/body) available in sidebar
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add image upload and manipulation to editor
userStories.description: Add image handling to the Fabric.js editor. Implement: image upload from local files, image placement on canvas with aspect ratio preservation, image manipulation (crop via clipPath, filters via Fabric.js built-in filters — brightness, contrast, saturation, blur), resize with corner handles. Image library panel in sidebar for uploaded images.
acceptanceCriteria: Images uploadable via file picker and drag-drop
Images placed on canvas preserving aspect ratio
Crop tool using Fabric.js clipPath
Filter controls (brightness, contrast, saturation) in property panel
Resize via corner handles maintains aspect ratio when shift held
Uploaded images shown in sidebar library panel
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add shapes and layers to image editor
userStories.description: Add shape tool and layer management to the Fabric.js editor. Shapes: config-driven definitions (rectangle, circle, triangle, star, arrow, etc.) following the Canva clone pattern. Layer panel: list all canvas objects with drag-to-reorder, visibility toggle, lock toggle, delete. Selection: multi-select with shift-click, group/ungroup operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Shape tool adds configurable shapes from config-driven definitions
At least 10 shape types available
Layer panel lists all objects with thumbnails
Drag-to-reorder changes z-index
Visibility and lock toggles per layer
Multi-select with shift-click
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add template system and export to image editor
userStories.description: Add template save/load and export to the image editor. Templates: save current canvas state as JSON template, load templates from library, default starter templates for common formats (Instagram post 1080x1080, Story 1080x1920, LinkedIn 1200x627, Twitter 1600x900, Blog header 1200x630). Export: PNG, JPG (via canvas.toDataURL), PDF (via jsPDF with canvas render). Multi-page support for carousel/multi-image exports.
acceptanceCriteria: Save current design as reusable template
Template library with default format presets
Export to PNG and JPG with quality control
Export to PDF via jsPDF
Format presets for major platforms (IG, LinkedIn, Twitter, Blog)
Exported images saved as derived_assets in ContentOS
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add content calendar view
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/app/calendar/page.tsx with a monthly/weekly content calendar. Display scheduled queue slots, published content, and draft content as cards on the calendar. Use date-fns for date math. Calendar cells show: content node title, target platform icons, status badge (draft/scheduled/published). Click to open content detail. Drag-drop to reschedule (updates scheduledFor on queue slot).
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar page renders monthly and weekly views
Queue slots displayed as cards on scheduled dates
Published content shown with platform icons
Click card opens content detail
Drag-drop reschedules queue slots
Month/week toggle and date navigation
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add content pipeline kanban view
userStories.description: Create apps/content-os/src/app/pipeline/page.tsx with a kanban board showing content lifecycle. Columns: Idea → Draft → Review → Scheduled → Published. Cards show content node summary, asset count, target platforms. Drag between columns updates content status. Filter by platform, content type, date range.
acceptanceCriteria: Kanban board with 5 lifecycle columns
Content cards show title, asset count, platform badges
Drag between columns updates content_node status
Filter controls for platform and content type
Card click opens content detail
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add multi-platform preview grid
userStories.description: Create a preview component that shows how content will look on each target platform — inspired by the Planner grid pattern. Show platform-specific formatting: character limits, image dimensions, link preview behavior. Render side-by-side: Instagram post mockup, LinkedIn post mockup, Twitter/X mockup, Blog preview. Each mockup uses platform-appropriate styling and truncation rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview grid shows content rendered per platform
Instagram mockup with correct aspect ratio and caption truncation
LinkedIn mockup with headline and body formatting
Twitter/X mockup with character count and thread detection
Blog mockup with title, featured image, excerpt

userStories.title: Add video timeline editor to Studio
userStories.description: Replace the stub in apps/studio/src/app/console/page.tsx with a basic video timeline editor. Use FFmpeg.wasm for client-side video processing. Implement: video preview player, timeline scrubber with frame markers, trim controls (set in/out points), cut at playhead. Store edit decisions as EDL (edit decision list) — non-destructive. Render final video via FFmpeg.wasm when user exports.
acceptanceCriteria: Video player with playback controls at /console
Timeline scrubber with visual waveform or frame thumbnails
In/out point trim controls
Cut at playhead splits segment
Edit decisions stored as non-destructive EDL
Export renders final video via FFmpeg.wasm
TypeScript compiles with no errors

userStories.title: Add Studio compilation export to Content OS
userStories.description: Create apps/studio/src/app/api/compilations/[id]/export/route.ts. Load compilation and clips from Studio DB. Create content_node in Content OS (type='video'). Create derived_assets for each clip. Add 'Export to Content OS' button in compilation detail UI.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/compilations/[id]/export endpoint in Studio
Creates content_node record in shared Supabase
Each clip becomes derived_asset linked to content_node
Export button in compilation detail UI

userStories.title: Disable Vercel deployment protection for production
userStories.description: Update Vercel project settings to disable deployment protection on production deployments so the app is publicly accessible. Or configure a custom domain. Currently both apps return Vercel SSO auth page.
acceptanceCriteria: Production deployment accessible without Vercel SSO
App loads correctly in browser
API routes accessible for client requests

userStories.title: Fix Coolify Supabase health to running state
userStories.description: The Supabase stack on Coolify shows 'Degraded (unhealthy)'. Investigate which services are failing (Kong, GoTrue, PostgREST, etc.) and fix. The database itself is working but the API gateway may not be routing correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Coolify shows Supabase service as Healthy
Kong API gateway responds on port 8000
GoTrue auth service responds
PostgREST API service responds
